fix.description: Root cause: on every 5th time you reopened the app, it auto-asked for a rating via Google's in-app-review API. That API throws the native 'Something went wrong — check Google Play / reinstall' popup when the app wasn't installed from the Play Store — which is exactly the case for the alpha/test builds you install. (Same Google-Play family as the Rate-the-App issue 4664.) Fix: removed that automatic on-open rating trigger. Production users installed from the Play Store still get a rating nudge after a positive action (logging a weight/workout/meal), and the manual 'Rate the App' button opens your store page directly. This is native-only behaviour (the popup can't occur in a browser), so it's verified by the code change + a clean app boot with no errors (screenshot). On your next alpha build you should no longer see this popup on app open.
